questions.prompt: Which word means the son of your brother or sister?
questions.explanation: Your nephew is the son of your brother or sister; the daughter would be your niece. A cousin is the child of your aunt or uncle, and a grandfather is your parent's father. Nephew (boy) and niece (girl) are a gender pair — keep them apart.
Pilot, baker and engineer are all jobs — work that people do. Black is a colour, so it is the odd one out. Grouping words by field (jobs, colours, foods) is a useful memory trick.

questions.prompt: Complete the common phrase: "a ___ of juice".
questions.explanation: Juice is a drink you pour, so we say a bottle of juice (or a glass of juice). A loaf, a slice and a piece all go with solid foods like bread or cake — they cannot hold a liquid like juice.

The opposite of early (before the expected time) is late (after the expected time): I am early / I am late. Fast is the opposite of slow, new is the opposite of old, and cheap is the opposite of expensive — none of those means the same as late.

questions.prompt: Which word belongs to the field "colours"?
questions.explanation: Pink is a colour. Cousin is a family word, pilot is a job, and Friday is a day of the week, so none of those is a colour. Colours at A1 include red, blue, green, yellow, orange, black, white, brown, grey and pink.

questions.prompt:
Read: "My father has one brother. That brother has a daughter named Yasmine."
What is Yasmine to me?
questions.explanation: My father's brother is my uncle, and the child of my uncle is my cousin. Yasmine is a girl, but cousin is the same word for boys and girls. A niece would be my own sibling's daughter, a sister shares my parents, and an aunt is a parent's sister — so only cousin fits the daughter of an uncle.
